Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.

- World model: - **Player:** Magenta-bordered white square at rows 36-41, cols 11-16 (center ~38-39, 13-14) - **Room:** Charcoal interior (rows 12-41, cols 15-44) with black exterior - **Targets on right wall:** Red (R) rows 19-22, Blue (b) rows 25-28, Light Green (N) rows 31-34 — all at cols 42-45 - **Left wall markers:** Gray (g) blocks at rows 14-15, 20-21, 26-27, 32-33 - **Bottom panel:** Shows collection order — Pink, Red, Green, Blue - **Path:** White/gray blocks extend right from player (rows 38-39, cols 16-22)
- Goal model: Collect colored blocks in the order shown at bottom (pink→red→green→blue). Pink is already the player. Next target is Red.
- Plan: Move player UP to align with Red block (rows 19-22), then extend path RIGHT to connect.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! Now I see what happened:
- Player is at rows 30-35
- Red block is now at rows 31-34, cols 36-39 (it moved! It was at cols 42-45 before)
- Green block is at rows 31-34, cols 42-45
- Path extends to col 40
So when the player moved down, the red block moved with the path! The red block is now "attached" to the end of the path. The path goes from the player (col 16) to col 40, and the red block is at cols 36-39, which is part of the path.
Wait, that doesn't make sense. Let me re-read: the red block is at cols 36-39, and the path extends to col 40. So the red block is embedded in the path.
Actually, looking at the image, I see the path extends from the player to the green block, with a red segment at the end. The red block seems to have been "collected" and is now part of the path.
So the mechanic is:
1. When the path reaches a target block, that block gets "collected" and becomes part of the path
2. The collected block moves with the path when the player moves
3. The path needs to reach the next target block to collect it
Now the path extends to col 40, and the green block is at cols 42-45. I need to extend the path 2 more columns to reach the green block. Let me try RIGHT.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Path extends to col 40, green block at cols 42-45
# Need to extend path to reach green block
# Each RIGHT extends ~6 cols, but we only need ~2 more cols
# Let me try one RIGHT to see if it reaches

action(['RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
